title: Fix crash when handling empty adb key list
body:
This PR fixes a crash in the ADB module when no ADB keys are parsed. The code path that processes the ADB key list previously assumed that the parsed data would always contain a non-empty user_keys list, which could lead to a crash when the list is missing or empty. The fix ensures we default to an empty list and always return an empty user_keys list instead of crashing.

Planned changes:
- Update the ADB dumpsys artifact parsing to gracefully handle missing/empty user_keys by defaulting to an empty list and avoiding iterating over None.
- Ensure self.results always contains a valid structure even when no keys are present.

step_plan.summary: Fix crash in ADB dumpsys artifact parsing by safely handling empty or missing ADB keys and ensuring a valid results structure.

steps.operation: Guard against None/missing user_keys in DumpsysADBArtifact.parse
steps.reason: Previously, the function iterated over parsed.get('user_keys') assuming a non-None iterable. If no keys are present, this can raise a TypeError and crash. By defaulting to an empty list (e.g., parsed.get('user_keys', [])) and building the results from that, we ensure a robust path even when there are no keys.
steps.side_effects: Prevents crashes when there are no ADB keys; downstream code will always receive a valid results structure. Tests should be updated to include an empty-key scenario; potential minor behavioral change if any downstream logic previously relied on None vs empty list; no external API changes.
